2606.06915.title: ThinkBooster: A Unified Framework for Seamless Test-Time Scaling of LLM Reasoning
2606.06915.paper_summary: Test-time compute (TTC) scaling has emerged as a powerful paradigm for improving large language model (LLM) reasoning by allocating additional compute during inference, e.g., via multi-sample generation and verifier-based reranking. Existing TTC scaling strategies and reasoning scorers remain fragmented, evaluated under inconsistent protocols, and are rarely analyzed through the lens of quality-cost trade-offs. We introduce ThinkBooster, a unified framework for seamless test-time compute scaling of LLM reasoning, which consists of (i) a modular Python library implementing state-of-the-art TTC scaling strategy and scorer families, (ii) a benchmark that jointly evaluates performance and computational efficiency, and (iii) a deployable OpenAI-compatible proxy service that enables drop-in integration of adaptive reasoning into real-world applications. We further provide a demo visual debugger for inspecting the reasoning trajectories, intermediate selection decisions, and alternative reasoning paths. Empirical results on mathematical and coding tasks reveal the performance-compute trade-offs of TTC scaling strategies and scoring methods and demonstrate that ThinkBooster provides practical gains in real-world tasks. The code is available online under an MIT license.
2606.06915.paper_summary_zh: 測試時計算（TTC）擴展已成為改善大型語言模型（LLM）推理的強大範式，通過在推理過程中分配額外的計算資源，例如，通過多樣本生成和基於驗證者的重新排序。現有的TTC擴展策略和推理評分器仍然是零散的，在不一致的協議下進行評估，並且很少通過質量-成本權衡的視角進行分析。我們介紹了ThinkBooster，這是一個統一的框架，用於無縫的LLM推理測試時計算擴展，該框架由以下部分組成：（i）一個模塊化的Python庫，實現了最先進的TTC擴展策略和評分器系列，（ii）一個共同評估性能和計算效率的基準，以及（iii）一個可部署的OpenAI兼容代理服務，該服務使自適應推理能夠無縫整合到現實世界的應用中。我們還提供了一個演示可視化調試器，用於檢查推理軌跡、中間選擇決策和替代推理路徑。在數學和編碼任務上的實證結果揭示了TTC擴展策略和評分方法的性能-計算權衡，並證明ThinkBooster在現實世界任務中提供了實際收益。該代碼在網上以MIT許可證提供。
2606.06915.author: Vladislav Smirnov et.al.
2606.06915.authors: Vladislav Smirnov, Chieu Nguyen, Sergey Senichev, Minh Ngoc Ta, Ekaterina Fadeeva, Artem Vazhentsev, Daria Galimzianova, Nikolai Rozanov, Viktor Mazanov, Jingwei Ni, Tianyi Wu, Igor Kiselev, Mrinmaya Sachan, Iryna Gurevych, Preslav Nakov, Timothy Baldwin, Artem Shelmanov
